mapping.a_element: predicted mass/number distribution of galaxy clusters (cluster abundance)

brief_justification: Only the target quantity is superficially similar (a distribution over the mass of large clumps); the machinery differs entirely. Paper A derives cluster abundances from the statistics of the primordial density field (mass variance sigma_8, non-Gaussian chi^2 field, early dark energy) via cosmological structure-formation/mass-function calculations, whereas Paper B solves the Smoluchowski binary-coagulation kinetic equation using Zakharov transformations to obtain a constant-mass-flux Kolmogorov cascade with a gelation threshold set by mass capacity. There is no coagulation kernel, mass flux, or Zakharov/cascade structure in A and no primordial-fluctuation statistics in B, so no role-for-role mapping of the governing process holds.
